method_description: The n-octanol/PBS partitioning method for logD₇.₄ determination: Dilute 50 μl radiotracer (75–300 kBq/ml) to 500 μl with pH 7.4 PBS, mix with equal n-octanol, vortex 3 min, centrifuge at 10,000×g for 6 min. Collect 100 μl from each phase, perform γ-counting, calculate lg (octanol/PBS counts). Run in quadruplicate, express as mean ± SD.
Previously described protocols were followed for determination of integrin affinities by ELISA on immobilized integrins.

[68Ga]NODAGA-RGD (1 MBq/ml) was incubated in fresh human serum at 37°C for 30, 60, 120, and 180 min to conduct protein binding studies. The mixture was then processed through a size exclusion spin column, and protein binding was quantified by measuring the radioactivity retained on the column and in the eluate using a gamma counter.

96-well plates were coated with αvβ3 integrins and incubated with a mixture of 125I-echistatin and gradient concentrations of the target peptide. Unbound radioligand was removed by washing, and receptor-bound radioactivity was eluted from the plates with 2 M sodium hydroxide solution.

1.0 mL PBS (pH 7.4) and 1.0 mL 1-octanol were mixed. Then, 5 uL of 68Ga-NGR-RGD (740 kBq), 68Ga-RGD (740 kBq) or 68Ga-NGR (740 kBq) was respectively added to the mixed solution. The mixture was then continuously vortexed for 5 min and centrifuged (4000 rpm, 5 min). 100 µL samples of each phase were measured by gamma counter. The partition coefficient was calculated as follows: LogP = Log10 (counts in 1-octanol/counts in PBS).

Log P was determined by dissolving the radiotracer in equal volumes of n-octanol and 25 mM phosphate buffer (pH 7.4). After mixing and centrifugation, radioactivity in both phases was measured in triplicate with a γ-counter, and the partition coefficient was calculated.

[67Ga]CG6 or [67Ga]CD6 was added into an equal volume (500 µL:500 µL) mixture of n-octanol and 25 mM phosphate buffer (pH7.4). After vigorous shaKing for 30 minutes, the mixture was centrifuged at 5,000 rpm for 5 minutes. Samples from both n-octanol and aqueous layers were obtained, and their radioactivity was measured. The partition coefficients (Log P-values) were calculated as the average of three independent measurements.

The affinity of [natGa]CD6 to αvβ6 integrin was evaluated by competitive binding assay using [125I]IFMDV2 as a radioligand (n = 4). The Ki value of [natGa]CD6 was calculated by using GraphPad Prism 7 .

The octanol/water partition coeﬃcient was determined as previously described. Brieﬂy, a 50 μL aliquot from a 0.15 MBq/mL stock of the radiotracer was diluted with PBS (450 μL, pH 7.4) and combined with 500 μL of n-octanol in microfuge tubes (n = 4/tracer). After vortexing for 3 min, the tubes were centrifuged (10,000g; 6 min), and 100 μL aliquots of the PBS and noctanol phases were counted in a γ-counter. Data are expressed as mean ± standard deviation.

The octanol/water partition coeﬃcient was determined as previously described. Brieﬂy, a 50 μL aliquot from a 0.15 MBq/mL stock of the radiotracer was diluted with PBS (450 μL, pH 7.4) and combined with 500 μL of n-octanol in microfuge tubes (n = 4/tracer). After vortexing for 3 min, the tubes were centrifuged (10,000g; 6 min), and 100 μL aliquots of the PBS and noctanol phases were counted in a γ-counter. Data are expressed as mean ± standard deviation.

Log P was determined by dissolving the compound in equal volumes (3 mL:3 mL) of n-octanol and 25 mM phosphate buffer (pH 7.4). After vigorous stirring and centrifugation, radioactivity in both layers was measured in triplicate using a γ-counter.

The n-octanol/PBS partitioning method for logD₇.₄ determination: Dilute 50 μl radiotracer (75–300 kBq/ml) to 500 μl with pH 7.4 PBS, mix with equal n-octanol, vortex 3 min, centrifuge at 10,000×g for 6 min. Collect 100 μl from each phase, perform γ-counting, calculate lg (octanol/PBS counts). Run in quadruplicate, express as mean ± SD.
Competitive binding enzyme-linked immunosorbent assays (ELISAs) for integrins αvβ6 and αvβ3 followed previously described procedures and were analyzed using Prism software.

The lipophilicity was measured by the “shake-flask” method.

Cells were seeded in 24-well plates (105 cells per well) 24 h ahead of the experiment. Before the experiment, cells were washed with 1 mL HBSS twice and 0.5 mL binding media (HBSS with 0.1 % BSA and 1 mM Mn2+) was added to each well. Then blocKing agent (10 μg corresponding cold peptide for the 64Cu-labeled conjugates) was added to half of the wells as cold block to determine in vitro non-specific binding, followed by 64Cu-labeled conjugates in increased concentra tions. After incubation of samples for 2 h at 4 ◦C, the cells were rinsed with cold binding buffer (1 mL) twice and lysed with 0.5 % SDS solution. The cell lysates were collected and measured in a gamma counter. The protein content of each cell lysate sample was determined by Pierce BCA Protein Assay Kit. The measured radioactivity associated with each cell lysate sample was normalized to the amount of cell protein present (fmol/mg).

The lipophilicity was measured by the “shake-flask” method. The 64Cu-labeled conjugate (10 μL) was added to a solution of PBS (500 μL, pH 7.4) and 1-octanol (500 μL). After shook for 30 min to reach equi librium, the resulting mixture was centrifuged for 10 min at 70g. For each phase, an aliquot (10 μL, n =5) was added to a tube and counted in a gamma-counter. Caution: be careful to cross contaminate between the two phases. The partition coefficient was calculated as the average log ratio of the radioactivity (CPM value) in the 1-octanol fraction and PBS fraction

An aliquot (74 kBq) of [68Ga]Ga-DOTA-FAPI-RGD was added into a 15 mL centrifuge tube containing 5 mL PBS (pH =7.4) and 5 mL n-octanol. The mixed solution was shaken at room temperature for 5 min before centrifugation (10000 rpm, 5 min). Three samples were collected from each phase for γ-counter analysis

The mixed solution was shaken at room temperature for 5 min before centrifugation (10000 rpm, 5 min). Three samples were collected from each phase for γ-counter analysis
The partition coefficient of the radiolabeled compounds was determined in a mixture of PBS (pH 7.4) and n-octanol.

The octanol-water partition coefficient (LogP) was determined by partitioning between 1-octanol and PBS (n=3). BALB/C nude mice were used to establish LS174T, SW620 and HT29 colorectal cancer xenograft models, and radioactivity was measured using a well-type γ counter.
The partition coefficient of the radiolabeled compounds was determined in a mixture of PBS (pH 7.4) and n-octanol based on our previously published papers.

The partition coefficient (logD) of the radiotracer 68Ga-FAP-RGD was determined using a standard n-octanol/water two-phase system, a classic method for evaluating the lipophilic-hydrophilic properties of pharmaceutical and nuclear medicine agents.
1-Octanol/water partition assay (pH 7.4) was performed to determine lipophilicity as logD.
In vitro binding affinities were acquired by competitive displacement experiments, each performed in triplicate.
The water pH7.4/1-octanol partition coefficient (logD(7.4)) of the developed radiotracers was determined by shake-flask method

The 50% inhibitory concentration (IC50) values for each compound were obtained by nonlinear regression analysis using GraphPad Prism Software (v5.04). Each experiment was performed at least three times, each experiment being performed in triplicate.

Radiolabelling with 68Ga was done as described above, and a solution containing 15 MBq in 1.0 mL was prepared by dilution with distilled H2O. Eleven inhibitor (unlabelled peptide) concentrations were tested to estimate the IC50 value at 0.1, 0.5, 1, 5, 10, 50, 100, 250, 500, 1000, and 5000 nM.

Radiolabelling with 68Ga was done as described above, and a solution containing 15 MBq in 1.0 mL was prepared by dilution with distilled H2O. Eleven inhibitor (unlabelled peptide) concentrations were tested to estimate the IC50 value at 0.1, 0.5, 1, 5, 10, 50, 100, 250, 500, 1000, and 5000 nM.

Approximately 370 kBq of the radiopeptide was dissolved in 500 μL of normal saline and mixed with an equal volume of n-octanol in a centrifuge tube.After mixing at room temperature for 10 min, the mixture was centrifuged at 14,000 rpm for 5 min to achieve phase separation.Aliquots of 100 μL each were taken from the organic phase (n-octanol) and the aqueous phase, and their radioactivities were measured using a γ-counter.The experiment was repeated three times, the partition coefficient was calculated and its logarithm was obtained as the logP value.
The shake-flask method is the gold standard for measuring logP/logD, ideal for assessing hydrophilic/lipophilic properties of peptides, radiolabeled agents and organics.
The shake-flask method is the gold standard for measuring logP/logD, ideal for assessing hydrophilic/lipophilic properties of peptides, radiolabeled agents and organics.

1–2 MBq of the radiotracer (dissolved in 5 μl of labeling buffer) was added to a mixed system consisting of 495 μl of pH 7.4 PBS (aqueous phase) and 500 μl of n-octanol (organic phase).The mixture was shaken at room temperature for 30 minutes and then centrifuged at 13,200 rpm for 5 minutes to separate the two phases.100 μl aliquots were taken from each phase, and the radioactivity was measured using a gamma counter. The logarithm of the concentration ratio of the two phases was calculated, .

1–2 MBq of the radiotracer (dissolved in 5 μl of labeling buffer) was added to a mixed system consisting of 495 μl of pH 7.4 PBS (aqueous phase) and 500 μl of n-octanol (organic phase).The mixture was shaken at room temperature for 30 minutes and then centrifuged at 13,200 rpm for 5 minutes to separate the two phases.100 μl aliquots were taken from each phase, and the radioactivity was measured using a gamma counter. The logarithm of the concentration ratio of the two phases was calculated.

1–2 MBq of the radiotracer (dissolved in 5 μL of labeling buffer) was added to a biphasic system containing 495 μL of phosphate-buffered saline (PBS, pH 7.4) as the aqueous phase and 500 μL of n-octanol as the organic phase.The mixture was vortexed/shaken at room temperature for 30 minutes to reach phase equilibrium, then centrifuged at 13,200 rpm for 5 minutes to achieve complete phase separation.100 μL aliquots were sampled from both the aqueous and organic phases, respectively. The radioactivity of each aliquot was quantified using a gamma counter.The octanol/PBS partition coefficient (logD₇.₄) was calculated as the logarithm (base 10) of the ratio of the radiotracer concentration in the n-octanol phase to that in the PBS (pH 7.4) phase. All experiments were performed in triplicate to ensure the reliability of the results.
This method measures the Ccell​/Cmedium​ partition coefficient of ⁹⁹ᵐTc-labeled RGD/E peptides in HUVE cells at 4°C (to inhibit active transport).

SE HPLC-purified ⁹⁹ᵐTc-RGD or ⁹⁹ᵐTc-RGE was added to HUVE cell suspension (2×10⁶~3×10⁶ cells/mL) in PBS/EBM/EGM medium. After incubation at 4°C for 30 min (to inhibit active transport and non-specific endocytosis), centrifugation was performed at 2000 rpm for 4 min. Radioactivity of 30 μL supernatant and washed cell pellets was measured separately, and the partition coefficient was calculated as their ratio.

Mix equal volumes of n-octanol and buffered aqueous phase, add the analyte and shake thoroughly until phase equilibrium is reached. After separating the two phases, quantify the analyte concentration in each phase via UV-Vis spectroscopy, high-performance liquid chromatography, liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry or other methods. Calculate P as the ratio of the analyte concentration in n-octanol to that in the aqueous phase, then take the base-10 logarithm to obtain logP.

The purified [ 68Ga]Ga-DOTAGA-LacN(NAP) (50 µL, 2–3 MBq) was diluted with 450 µL water, and then 500 µL n-octanol was added to, respectively. The mixtures were shaken vigorously for 5 min and centrifugated (9000 rpm) for 5 min. The radioactivity of the n-octanol phases (20 µL) and aqueous phases (20 µL) was determined by gamma counter.

The mixtures were shaken vigorously for 5 min and centrifugated (9000 rpm) for 5 min. The radioactivity of the n-octanol phases (20 µL) and aqueous phases (20 µL) was determined by gamma counter.

100 μl of [ 99mTc]Tc-M-7 (20–30 MBq) was added in a tube that contains 1 mL of n-octanol and 1 mL of water. The tube was vortexed vigorously for 10 min and then centrifuged for 5 min at 4000 g. We sampled 100 μL of three aliquots from each layer and counted them by γ-counter. Finally, we calculated the partition ratios by Log (counts in organic phase/ counts in aqueous phase).

Affinity was measured on CD11b⁺ RAW264.7 cells via pretargeted binding and saturation binding assays. For pretargeted binding, cells were incubated with anti-CD11b-TCO/anti-CD11b, then Tz-radioligand, with radioactivity detected by γ-counting (experimental, blocKing and control groups set). Saturation binding used gradient concentrations of ⁶⁸Ga/NOTA-anti-CD11b and ⁸⁹Zr/DFO-anti-CD11b. Nonspecific binding was corrected with excess unlabeled anti-CD11b, and GraphPad software calculated Kd and receptor density from specific binding curves (triplicate experiments).

The n-octanol/PBS partition coefficient (Log D) assessed the hydrophilicity/hydrophobicity of ⁶⁸Ga-NOTA-polypeptide-PEG₁₁-Tz. 10 μL of the radioligand was added to a two-phase system of 500 μL pH 7.4 PBS and 500 μL n-octanol, vortexed vigorously for 1 min at room temperature, then centrifuged at 12000 rpm for 5 min. Radioactivity of both phases was measured by γ-counting. Log D was calculated as the log of the radioactivity ratio of n-octanol to aqueous phase, with all procedures performed in triplicate.

The Log D7.4​ (distribution coefficient at pH 7.4) was determined via the n-octanol/25 mM phosphate buffer system. Purified 99mTc-labeled probes were added to pre-equilibrated two phases, vortexed and centrifuged. 1 mL aliquots of each phase were taken to measure radioactivity with a gamma counter. Log D7.4​ was the log of the radioactivity ratio of octanol to buffer.

Competitive binding assays were performed using U87MG cells, and cell-associated radioactivity was measured with a γ-counter. The IC50 value was calculated by nonlinear regression using Kaleidagraph.

Common methods for IC50 determination involve preparing serial dilutions of the inhibitor. In cell-based assays using CCK-8 or MTT, cells are seeded, treated with gradient concentrations, incubated, and detected for absorbance.
The octanol-water partition coefficient (Log P) measures lipophilicity by quantifying compound distribution between n-octanol and water phases.
The octanol-water partition coefficient (Log P) measures lipophilicity by quantifying compound distribution between n-octanol and water phases.

Dissolve approx. 1.5MBq of 68Ga-NOTA-c(NGR) in 10μL aqueous solution and add to an Eppendorf tube containing 0.5mL PBS (pH 7.4) and 0.5mL 1octanol. Vigorously shake the mixture for 20min, then centrifuge at 20000rpm for 5min for complete layer separation. Aspirate 100μL from each layer into separate tubes. Measure radioactivity with a gamma counter.

The test compound is added to an immiscible n-octanol-water two-phase system and thoroughly shaken at a constant temperature until partition equilibrium is achieved. The equilibrium concentrations of the compound in the lipid phase (n-octanol phase) and the aqueous phase are then determined, and the common logarithm of the ratio of these two concentrations is calculated to obtain the lipophilicity coefficient (logP).

The 64Cu radiotracer was purified by HPLC, and volatiles were completely removed under vacuum. The residue was dissolved in an equal-volume mixture (30mL:3mL) of n octanol and 25mM phosphate buffer (pH7.4). After vigorous stirring for ～20min, the mixture was centrifuged at 8000 rpm for 5 min. Triplicate samples from the n-octanol and aqueous layers were counted using γ-counter. The Log P value was determined in three independent experiments and is reported as mean±SD.

The 64Cu radiotracer was purified by HPLC, and volatiles were completely removed under vacuum. The residue was dissolved in an equal-volume mixture (30mL:3mL) of n octanol and 25mM phosphate buffer (pH7.4). After vigorous stirring for ～20min, the mixture was centrifuged at 8000 rpm for 5 min. Triplicate samples from the n-octanol and aqueous layers were counted using γ-counter. The Log P value was determined in three independent experiments and is reported as mean±SD.

To a mixture of 300μL no ctanol and 300μL phosphate-buffered saline (PBS, pH 7.4), 30μL of purified radiolabeled RGD derivative was added and vortexed. The mixture was centrifuged at 6000 rpm for 10 min at 4 °C. Aliquots from both aqueous and octanol layers were collected, and the activity of each sample was measured using a γ counter. The Log P value was calculated as the logarithm of the ratio of radioactivity in the organic phase to that in the aqueous phase (mean of three replicates).

Affinity assays were performed in U87MG cells, and radioactivity was measured using a γ-counter. All experiments were performed in duplicate or triplicate. IC50 values were determined by nonlinear regression

U87MG cells (2×10⁵/well, high αvβ3) were seeded overnight. After washing, cells were incubated with 125I-echistatin and varying concentrations (0-1000 nM) of ⁿᵃᵗCu-NODAGA-c(RGDfK) for 2h at RT. The partition coefficient (Log P) was determined using the shake‑flask method. Equal volumes of n-octanol and water were mixed and shaken for 24 h to mutually saturate the two phases, followed by standing for phase separation. Then 0.5 mL of each phase was transferred into a microtube, and 100 μL of the labeled peptide was added. The mixture was vigorously agitated and centrifuged at 3000 rpm for 5 min to separate the layers. Three aliquots (100 μL) were taken from each phase, and the radioactivity was measured using a γ-counter. The Log P value was calculated as the logarithm of the ratio of the average radioactivity in the n-octanol phase to that in the aqueous phase.

To measure IC50 via competitive binding assay with LNCaP cells, seed 5×10⁴ cells/well and adhere overnight. Wash with ice-cold PBS, incubate with fixed 68Ga-PSMA-11 (saturating dose) and gradient unlabeled competitor (0.01–1000 nM) at 4℃ for 2 h in dark. Wash, solubilize cells, measure radioactivity, calculate specific binding, fit to sigmoidal curve, and determine IC50.

Prepare a biphasic system in a 1.5 mL centrifuge tube with 0.5 mL normal saline (aqueous phase) and 0.5 mL n-octanol (organic phase) at 1:1 volume ratio. Add 3 渭L radioactive probe, vortex vigorously for 5 min to reach partition equilibrium, then centrifuge at 10000 r/min for 5 min for phase separation. Collect 0.1 mL supernatant from each phase, measure radioactivity via a gamma counter, and repeat the assay 5 times for average calculation.

Cellular uptake and blockage assays assessed the specific PSMA-binding ability of two ⁶⁸Ga-labeled probes. ~2×10⁵ cells in 24-well plates were pre-treated, incubated with ~74 KBq probes for 15–120 min, then washed and lysed with 0.1 M NaOH. After centrifugation, a gamma counter measured radioactivity. Data were normalized as % ID/2×10⁵ cells ± SD, with all assays run in triplicate.

The partition coefficient of the 177Lu-labeled agents was determined in 1-octanol and phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) (pH 7.4). 1-Octanol (3 mL) and PBS (3 mL) were pipetted into a 15 mL test tube containing 370 kBq of the test compound. The test tube was vortexed for 2 min and then centrifuged (4000× g, 5 min). Aliquots (0.1 mL) from the 1-octanol and PBS phases were transferred into two test tubes for counting. The amount of radioactivity in each test tube was measured using the automated γ-counter. The partition coefficient was calculated using the following equation: Log Pow = log[counts1-octanol/countsPBS]

The shake flask method was used to test the Log P values of the radiotracers. PBS (0.025% mol/L, pH 7.4) was used overnight to presaturate the noctanol used in the experiment. In summary, 900 μL of PBS, 1 mL of n-octanol, and 100 μL of radiotracer were added to a centrifuge tube, and the mixture was shaken vigorously for 5 min. To achieve phase separation, the mixture was then centrifuged at 10,000 rpm for 15 min. Then, 500 μL samples of PBS and n-octanol were collected and analyzed via a γ-counter. To determine the distribution coefficient, the radioactivity counts in the two phases were compared. Log P = log (cpm in n-octanol/cpm in PBS) was used to calculate the results from three parallel experiments. Log P values were expressed as the means ± SDs.

To achieve phase separation, the mixture was then centrifuged at 10,000 rpm for 15 min. Then, 500 μL samples of PBS and n-octanol were collected and analyzed via a γ-counter. To determine the distribution coefficient, the radioactivity counts in the two phases were compared. Log P = log (cpm in n-octanol/cpm in PBS) was used to calculate the results from three parallel experiments. Log P values were expressed as the means ± SDs.

For radioligand binding assay in HCC1954 cells, cells were incubated with serial concentrations of radiolabeled ligand for 2 h at 37 °C under 5% CO₂. Unbound ligand was removed by cold PBS washing, and cell-associated radioactivity was measured with a gamma counter. Specific binding was obtained by subtracting non-specific binding (excess unlabeled ligand) from total binding. Kd​ and Bmax​ were calculated via non-linear regression of specific binding data using GraphPad Prism (mean ± SD, n=3)

For the octanol-water partition coefficient assay, equal volumes of n-octanol and aqueous probe solution were mixed and shaken at 37 °C to achieve partition equilibrium, then centrifuged for phase separation. The concentration of the analyte in each phase was quantified, and logP values were calculated as the logarithm of the ratio of analyte concentration in n-octanol to that in water, with results expressed as mean ± SD (n=3).

For saturation binding assay in SKBR3 cells, cells were incubated with serial concentrations of radiolabeled ligand for 2 h at 37 °C under 5% CO₂. Unbound ligand was washed off with cold PBS, and cell-associated radioactivity was measured by a gamma counter. Specific binding was obtained by subtracting non-specific binding (with excess unlabeled ligand) from total binding. Kd​ and Bmax​ were calculated via non-linear regression of specific binding data using GraphPad Prism, with results presented as mean ± SD (n=3).

LigandTracer real-time binding assays were performed on SKOV-3 cells in the presence of HAS to determine the equilibrium dissociation constant (Kd​). Radiolabeled ligands were added to the HAS-containing cell system, and real-time ligand-receptor binding dynamics were continuously monitored by the LigandTracer platform until binding equilibrium was reached. Specific binding data were fitted to a one-site binding model, with Kd​ values calculated via nonlinear regression analysis and results expressed as mean ± SD (n=3).

Kd​ and Bmax​ were calculated by non-linear regression of specific binding data using GraphPad Prism, with results presented as mean ± SD (n=3).

For octanol/PBS partition coefficient determination, equal volumes of n-octanol and PBS solution of the analyte were mixed and shaken at 37 °C to reach partition equilibrium, then centrifuged for complete phase separation. Nonlinear regression fitting generated concentration–inhibition curves to calculate IC50, the concentration inhibiting 50% specific binding, serving as a key affinity indicator.

To determine the partition coefficient (logP) , a compound was dissolved in a predefined mixture of n-octanol and water ( typically 1:1 volume ratio ) . The sample was incubated at room temperature with continuous shaKing to ensure equilibrium, then allowed to phase separate. The concentration of the compound in both phases was quantified using validated analytical methods ( e.g. , HPLC / UV ) . LogP was calculated as the decimal logarithm of the ratio between the compound’s equilibrium concentrations in n-octanol and water phases, respectively. This approach ensured a robust physicochemical measurement of the compound’s lipophilicity.

Saturation binding experiments were performed to determine Kd of [99mTc/99gTc]4 in PC3 cell membranes. Prepared PC3 membranes were incubated with serial dilutions of [99mTc/99gTc]4 at 25±1°C for 120 min to reach equilibrium. Unbound ligand was removed by filtration and washing; bound radioactivity was measured. Specific binding was calculated, and Kd (ligand concentration for 50% receptor binding) was obtained via nonlinear regression.

Saturation binding experiments were conducted in intact PC3 cells to determine the dissociation constant (Kd) of the test ligand. PC3 cells were seeded in plates and cultured to confluence. Serial dilutions of radiolabeled ligand (e.g., [99mTc/99gTc]4) were added to cells, incubated at 25±1°C for 120 min to reach equilibrium. Unbound ligand was removed by washing with ice-cold buffer; bound radioactivity was measured. Specific binding was calculated by subtracting non-specific binding, and Kd was derived via nonlinear regression of the saturation curve.

The most common logD measurement includes the shake-flask method, HPLC method, and in silico calculation. The shake-flask method is the gold standard: compound is added to a mixture of n-octanol and aqueous buffer (pH 7.4), incubated, centrifuged, and concentrations in both phases are measured. LogD is calculated as the log ratio of octanol-to-water concentration. The HPLC method uses retention time for fast estimation, while in silico tools predict logD from chemical structure directly.

PC3-PIP cells were used for the saturation binding assays. Twenty-four hours before the experiments, the PC3-PIP cells (200,000/well) were seeded in two 24-well plates. After incubation, the medium was removed and replaced with RPMI-1640 without FBS. Different concentrations of [18F]AlF-NNA8P1, [18F]AlF-NNB8P1, or [18F]AlF-NCB8P1 (0.157−400 nM) were added to wells, and each concentration group was added in triplicate. One of the plates was additionally supplemented with unlabeled PSMA-617 (10 μg/well) prior to the addition of radioligands to saturate the PSMA target, which was used for comparison to determine the nonspecific binding. After incubating at 37 °C for 30 min, the medium was discarded, and the cells were washed twice with cold PBS containing 0.2% BSA. Subsequently, the cells were lysed by adding 0.5 mL of 1 M NaOH to each well, and the radioactivity of the lysate was measured with a γ-counter. Thereafter, the Kd values were computed using GraphPad Prism 8.0 software.

A mixture of octanol and phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) (0.01 mol/L, pH = 7.4) was presaturated by combining equal volumes (V/V = 1:1) and allowing the mixture to stratify. In a 2 mL centrifuge tube, 100 μL of the radiotracer solution (approximately 0.37−2.96 MBq) was introduced, followed by 900 μL of PBS and 1 mL of octanol. The mixture was vigorously vortexed at room temperature for 1 min to ensure thorough mixing and then centrifuged at 12,000 rpm for 3 min. After phase separation, three to four aliquots (100 μL each) were collected from either the organic or aqueous phase and transferred into separate counting tubes. For the repeated extraction step, a 100 μL aliquot from the aqueous phase was combined with 900 μL of fresh PBS and 1 mL of octanol in a new tube, followed by performing the same vortexing, centrifugation, and sampling procedures. This entire process was replicated three times. The radioactivity in all samples was quantified by using a γ-counter. The octanol/water partition coefficient (Log P) was calculated using the formula: Log P = log[(mean radioactivity in octanol phase)/(mean radioactivity in PBSphase)], and the final result is presented as mean ± standard deviation(SD).

The enzyme inhibition Kinetic assay measures IC50 by evaluating inhibitor effects on purified enzyme activity. Gradient inhibitor concentrations are incubated with enzyme and substrate, and reaction rates are detected via fluorescence, absorbance or luminescence. Inhibition rates are calculated and fitted to a four-parameter logistic model to determine the concentration achieving 50% enzyme inhibition, reflecting direct target inhibition potency.

For the partition coefficient study, radiotracers were added to a 10 mL mixture (1:1, v/v) of n-octanol and PBS. The mixture was shaken vigorously for 5 min at room temperature and centrifuged (2000 rpm, 5 min). Aliquots (300 μL) from each phase were withdrawn and measured using a γ-counter (CAPRAC-R; Capintec, Inc., Ramsey, NJ, USA). The n-octanol/ PBS distribution coefficient was expressed as Log D.
